session: akiendelllinux_cc_0 — resumed via /compact from 2026-07-21-harbor-master-projector-and-citadel-model. A short, high-leverage DESIGN session that closed the one gate the prior slate named (the 'no state' placement question) and, in doing so, deepened what a charter IS. No new code built; two commits of resolved structure. Akien signaled convergence ('it looks sorted to me. this has been a wild ride') and asked for a system restart next. Closed by /saveslate.
author: CC
in_flight: NOTHING is mid-build — this was a design/resolution session. What MOVED to durable structure: (1) THE PLACEMENT GATE IS RESOLVED (Akien ruling). Everything shareable lives BESIDE THE CODE as git-JSON — `state`, `history`, AND `validations` (validations MOVE OUT of db_domain, next to the thing they validate / the `proofs/` they seal). The database ends up holding ONLY the graph trees (embeddings). The old CLAUDE.md line 'code only, no state, ever' meant RUNTIME/INSTANCE state (that stays in ~/.cairn/); build-provenance is knowledge, frozen at PROVED, and belongs beside the code (Law 5 extended — intent, its voyage, AND its proofs share the address). This REVERSED charter-state-history-split child (b) as originally filed. (2) THE FULL PARTITION (Akien-agreed): intent shares its IMPLEMENTATION's address — a CODE-SEAM has code -> cairn/; a CONCEPT-PIECE's implementation IS the prose -> CairnCommons/ (its intention + tickets + quorum-signature resolution co-locate there; different hands seal a concept than seal code). Partition question: does this intention have code? A code-seam ticket STAGES in CairnCommons/tickets/ then MIGRATES beside code to become its `history`; a concept-piece ticket never migrates (CairnCommons is already its berth). (3) WHAT A CHARTER IS, deepened: a component/folder hosts MANY intentions and tickets, so the charter is not one intention — it is their SUMMARY. Two cadences that do better SEPARATE by PROVENANCE: the charter beside code = the SUMMARIZED DESIGN (authored at the DESIGN level — our chats; the settled why+role; changes only when design shifts); `state` = COMPILED from the tickets (OPERATIONAL level; changes every resolution). These are the beside-code faces of the two-levels-that-don't-conflict; welding them is what let 'the charter become the ticket'. Full beside-code decomposition of a component: summarized-design (charter, authored) / state (compiled) / history (append-only voyage, frozen ex-tickets) / validations (beside the proofs). When a ticket proves out its voyage FREEZES into history (Law 7) and its result FOLDS INTO the charter. Recorded on tickets/charter-state-history-split.json (child b resolved + a `refinement_2026-07-21` key). Committed CairnCommons 2078747 (placement) + a850fe7 (charter=summarized-design cut); BOTH pushed. No cairn/ code changed this session.
next_direction: AKIEN NAMED IT: a SYSTEM RESTART is next, and on resume he wants to UPDATE THE DOCS he's building for himself to TAKE TO WORK (the take-to-work docs — pick this up first after restart). Beyond that, the placement ruling spawned/unblocked concrete work, none of it built yet: (A) SPAWNED SIBLING — migrate the `validations` table OUT of db_domain to beside-code git files (the projector's own validation, persisted to db_domain 2026-07-21T15:50:30, is the first to move); this SHRINKS db_domain's role to owning the graph trees / truly-relational data, and REVISES the standing-lesson gate (baked into charter-state-history-split + harbor-master: 'VALIDATION persisted via db_domain + grep back') — the LESSON holds (produced-but-not-durably-recorded didn't happen) but the TARGET becomes a beside-code git validation file. (B) charter-state-history-split child (c) — migrate existing charters to state/history (inference_domain LAST, by its own frozen ruling). (C) child (d) — rewrite CLAUDE.md's 'code only, no state, ever' -> runtime/instance state + the 'co-locates intention+why.json' line + MAP recompile; this is now LOAD-BEARING because that line is presently FALSE (we ruled state lives beside code). CC read: child (d) is the highest-leverage build (CLAUDE.md is read first; the line actively contradicts the ruling) — but the take-to-work docs are what AKIEN asked for first. Buildable anytime, no gate: web_server children a/b/c (untouched since 2026-07-20).
open_threads: THE CITADEL IS NOT A DELIVERABLE (correction this session): the intentions/ folder IS the citadel — 'a model like a gigantic molecule above my head that you and I grab hold of and tweak, just a metaphor' (Akien). There is NOTHING to build there and NOTHING to 'graduate'; CC's prior-slate framing ('graduate the citadel model to a formal commons intention') was reifying a metaphor into a deliverable (the CC-reifies / Cairn-flows reflex) and is STRUCK. The 'tweaking' is our design chats, which precipitate to files, which compile to the folder.

THE THREE-SCALE PROJECTOR RECURSION (the session's conceptual spine — CC framing, Akien 'it looks sorted to me'): the compile-a-view-over-an-append-only-source move recurs at three scales — TICKET (history -> bounded state window, the projector built 2026-07-21), COMPONENT (a component's many intentions+tickets -> the charter as summarized design), SYSTEM (all charters -> intentions/ as the citadel index). OPEN sub-question, NOT resolved: the middle row (the summarized-design charter) is HAND-AUTHORED today while the outer two are compiled — does the charter-summary eventually get COMPILED from the tickets too, or does it stay authored because it is the precipitate of design chats (the design level, above the operational)? Left open deliberately; guess-and-adjust (Law 3).

TWO LEVELS THAT DO NOT CONFLICT (carried from prior slate, now the LOAD-BEARING distinction behind the charter cut): DESIGN level = our chats (author + reconcile intentions; 'slowly improving it'; the result of our chats) ABOVE the OPERATIONAL level (compiled FROM the files; read + steered, NEVER hand-edited; no door except a file + recompile, Law 4). The charter=summarized-design/state split is literally these two levels wearing their beside-code faces (authored summary vs compiled state). Captured in CC memory intentions-folder-is-the-control-surface.

THE CS-CHALLENGE FRAMING (Akien: 'this is a big challenge to how we've always done it — Computer Science in general — I like it'). The honest version (CC, kept falsifiable per Law 3): the PRIMITIVES are mostly known — immutable/append-only log as truth, state as a discardable projection (event sourcing / CQRS / Datomic / git), colocation of concerns, provenance-as-the-seam. The BET that is novel-as-a-system: those primitives hold ALL THE WAY UP — through tickets, through charters, through the intentions themselves — with PHYSICS instead of discipline at every seam (Law 4, 'no other door' — drift-by-hand-edit made impossible, not discouraged), AND applied to the DESIGN RECORD itself, not just app data. FALSIFIER: the first place we are FORCED to hand-edit a compiled view or mutate a record of truth in place is where the bet breaks. Held this afternoon.

charter-state-history-split STATE: child (a) BUILT+PROVEN+persisted+committed+pushed (2026-07-21). Child (b) PLACEMENT now RESOLVED (this session). Remaining: (c) migrate existing charters, (d) CLAUDE.md/MAP recompile — parent stays at PROVEME, NOT PROVED until c/d land. Window rule DEFAULT_WINDOW=count(5) still a guess to adjust (Law 3).

VERIFY-BEFORE-ACTING (Law 3 — pointers, not proofs): pushed CairnCommons commits this session — 2078747 (placement resolution) + a850fe7 (charter=summarized-design cut). No cairn/ commits this session (no code changed). The projector's green VALIDATION still lives in the db_domain `validations` table (claim 'proof test_projector.py passes under python3') — it is the FIRST record slated to migrate OUT to beside-code git under the new ruling. node_classes/code-seam.json members_so_far honesty debt is STILL untouched (db_domain, inference_domain, web-server, harbor-master, charter-state-history-split absent) — flag if it should be maintained. harbor-master.json unchanged this session (still at TICKETME; its placement edge is now unblocked by the ruling but it still waits on the base-class emit-chokepoint).
